 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm
- Unit tests for parsing/validation logic; integration tests for non-interactive fallback
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:10.981531934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:47.247657776Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eb17","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:47.247630806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-i1kt","title":"Port rich.emoji: emoji codes + Emoji renderable","description":"Implement Python Rich emoji support.

- Emoji code replacement in strings: :smile:, :smile-emoji:, :smile-text: (with variant selectors) when Console emoji is enabled.
- Provide an Emoji renderable/type similar to Python rich.emoji.Emoji.
- Use the same emoji name->unicode mapping as Python Rich (Rich 13.9.4 rich/_emoji_codes.py).

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for replacement + variants + unknown code passthrough
- Console respects ConsoleBuilder::emoji(false)
- Conformance fixtures include at least one emoji-code case comparing against Python Rich
- Update docs (RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY/README if needed)
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:37:41.446947137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:59.790333391Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i1kt","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:14.440550508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-i1kt","title":"Port rich.emoji: emoji codes + Emoji renderable","description":"Implement Python Rich emoji support.

- Emoji code replacement in strings: :smile:, :smile-emoji:, :smile-text: (with variant selectors) when Console emoji is enabled.
- Provide an Emoji renderable/type similar to Python rich.emoji.Emoji.
- Use the same emoji name->unicode mapping as Python Rich (Rich 13.9.4 rich/_emoji_codes.py).

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for replacement + variants + unknown code passthrough
- Console respects ConsoleBuilder::emoji(false)
- Conformance fixtures include at least one emoji-code case comparing against Python Rich
- Update docs (RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY/README if needed)

 {"id":"bd-vqr2","title":"Fix AGENTS.md duplication + missing TODO section","description":"AGENTS.md currently contains duplicated sections (Beads integration/landing the plane) and ends with an empty 'Note on Built-in TODO Functionality' heading.
